name: Fuente la Gallina and Mojón de la Abadesa Loop
name_original: Fuente la Gallina y Mojón de la Abadesa — circular desde Alar del Rey por el Nacimiento del Canal de Castilla
description: Hike the moderate 6.7-mile Fuente la Gallina y Mojón de la Abadesa circular trail from Alar del Rey, exploring the Canal de Castilla's sourc
region_intro: Hiking around Alar Del Rey offers diverse landscapes, from unique geological formations to historic waterways. The region is characterized by the intricate karst formations of the Las Tuerces Natural Area, including Peña Mesa, and the significant Pisuerga River. It is also notable for being the "Kilometer Zero" of the historic Canal de Castilla, providing flat towpaths for easy exploration. Best hiking trails around Alar Del Rey The most popular hiking route is Las Tuerces and Horadada Canyon Loop, a 13.4 miles (21.5 km) trail that takes 6 hours 3 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the unique karst formations and a scenic canyon. Another top favourite among local hikers is Fuente la Gallina and Mojón de la Abadesa Loop, a moderate 6.7 miles (10.9 km) path. This route offers varied terrain and takes approximately 2 hours 59 minutes. Local hikers also love the Canal de Castilla Circular from Herrera de Pisuerga, a 4.8 miles (7.7 km) trail leading through the historic Canal de Castilla towpaths, often completed in about 1 hour 57 minutes. Hiking around Alar Del Rey is defined by unique karst formations, river valleys, and historic canal towpaths. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging mountain trails. The routes in Alar Del Rey are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 150 reviews. More than 300 hikers have used komoot to explore Alar Del Rey's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Alar Del Rey?
A: Alar Del Rey offers a diverse network of over 50 hiking routes, catering to various skill levels. You'll find everything from easy strolls along historic waterways to more challenging trails through unique geological formations.

Q: Are there any easy hiking routes suitable for families or beginners?
A: Yes, Alar Del Rey has 16 easy routes perfect for families or those seeking a relaxed walk. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1067701541/canal-de-castilla-circular-from-herrera-de-pisuerga" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal de Castilla Circular from Herrera de Pisuerga&lt;/a&gt;, which follows the historic towpaths and offers flat terrain.

Q: What kind of unique natural features can I expect to see on a hike?
A: The region is renowned for its distinctive karst formations, particularly within the Las Tuerces Natural Area. You can explore impressive rock formations like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4413098" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Mesa&lt;/a&gt; and the intricate 'Las Tuerces labyrinth', which are sculpted by millions of years of water erosion.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Alar Del Rey?
A: Many routes in Alar Del Rey are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1309682863/fuente-la-gallina-and-mojon-de-la-abadesa-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente la Gallina and Mojón de la Abadesa Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate circular path that provides varied terrain and scenic views.

Q: What historical attractions can I visit while hiking in the area?
A: Alar Del Rey is rich in cultural heritage. You can incorporate visits to sites like the Romanesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4848732" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa María la Real Monastery&lt;/a&gt; or the 12th-century &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3306399" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Santa Cecilia&lt;/a&gt;. The Canal de Castilla itself is a significant 18th-century engineering marvel, with Alar Del Rey marking its 'Kilometer Zero'.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Yes, for more experienced hikers, there are 6 difficult routes available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1067760816/las-tuerces-and-horadada-canyon-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Tuerces and Horadada Canyon Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 21.5 km trail that explores the unique karst landscape and a dramatic canyon.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Alar Del Rey?
A: The hiking routes in Alar Del Rey are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.5 stars from over 150 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse landscapes, from the unique rock formations to the peaceful canal paths, and the well-maintained trails.

Q: Can I find trails that explore the Palencian Romanesque heritage?
A: Absolutely. The region is part of the Camino Natural del Románico Palentino. A great route to experience this is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1314447436/palencian-romanesque-natural-trail-pena-pico-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palencian Romanesque Natural Trail: Peña Pico Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which combines natural beauty with architectural interest.

Q: Are there any routes that feature water bodies or reservoirs?
A: The Pisuerga River flows through the region, and Alar Del Rey is situated along its banks. The river is integral to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5838460" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguilar Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, which is part of the 'Ruta de los Pantanos' (Reservoir Route), offering scenic walks along its shores.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Alar Del Rey?
A: While the Palencia Mountain to the north can be snow-covered in winter, spring brings lush green landscapes, making it an excellent time for hiking. The diverse terrain means you can find suitable routes across different seasons, though checking local weather is always recommended.

Q: Are there any trails that offer views of the Pisuerga River?
A: Many trails in the Alar Del Rey area follow or offer views of the Pisuerga River, which is a central feature of the landscape. The historic Canal de Castilla, which diverts water from the Pisuerga, also provides excellent riverside walking opportunities.
tips: At this place the Canal de Castilla begins its journey - a masterpiece of Spanish hydraulic engineering from the 18th / 19th century. The canal is 207 km long. Here we can see the commemorative monolith for the 200 years of the North Branch of the canal, installed in 1991.

Fuente de la Gallina recreational area has benches and tables to eat or have a snack. Beautiful place that invites you to observe, rest, and walk along the path that leads the Pisuerga River. A quiet and charming landscape.
It is a limestone rock 120 cm high, which served for centuries to mark the property and jurisdiction limits of the convent of San Andrés de Arroyo. The place has an information panel.

faqs.question: How long does the circular hike from Alar del Rey typically take?
faqs.answer: This circular hike, covering approximately 10.85 km, typically takes around 3 hours to complete. This duration is for active hiking time and doesn't include stops for rest or enjoying the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this trail?
faqs.answer: Komoot rates this circular trail as moderate. While much of the path along the Canal de Castilla is relatively flat with very little elevation gain, the 'moderate' rating accounts for the overall distance and duration of the route. It's generally suitable for various fitness levels.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The trail largely follows the historic towpaths (caminos de sirga) alongside the Canal de Castilla, meaning you can expect relatively flat paths. These are often well-maintained, but can vary from compacted earth to gravel. While the overall elevation gain is minimal, the route's length contributes to its moderate classification.

faqs.question: Are there any specific landmarks or points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route is rich in historical and natural sights. You'll encounter the Source of the Canal de Castilla, marking the beginning of this impressive waterway. Other highlights include the pleasant Park and Fuente de la Gallina recreational area, and the ancient boundary marker, Mojón de la Abadesa. The path is also dotted with 18th and 19th-century hydraulic engineering structures like locks and old mills.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead in Alar del Rey?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas aren't detailed, Alar del Rey serves as the starting point for the Canal de Castilla. Look for available parking within the town, especially near the area of the Nacimiento del Canal de Castilla, which is a prominent landmark and likely to have visitor access points.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for dogs?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. The flat towpaths along the Canal de Castilla provide a good surface for walking with your canine companion. However, always ensure your dog is kept on a leash, especially in areas where wildlife might be present or near other hikers, and remember to carry waste bags.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery, with changing foliage or blooming wildflowers. Summer can be warm, so an early start is advisable. Winter hiking is possible, but be prepared for colder temperatures and potentially damp conditions along the canal.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike this trail along the Canal de Castilla. It is a publicly accessible route, allowing you to enjoy the historical and natural surroundings freely.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other paths. You'll find sections that overlap with the Camino del Besaya: Palencia, the Canal de Castilla (Ramal Norte-Sur), the GR-89 BTT Canal de Castilla (Ramal Norte Etapa Alar - Frómista), and the Camino Natural del Románico Palentino. Ruta del Carbón de cok. Etapa 1.

faqs.question: Are there facilities like cafes or pubs near the trailhead in Alar del Rey?
faqs.answer: Alar del Rey is a small town and, as the starting point of the Canal, it offers some local amenities. You should be able to find cafes or pubs in the town center where you can grab refreshments before or after your hike.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I spot along the Canal de Castilla?
faqs.answer: The tranquil environment along the Canal de Castilla provides opportunities for wildlife spotting, particularly various bird species that inhabit the waterway and surrounding areas. Keep an eye out for waterfowl, raptors, and other birds, especially in quieter sections of the trail.
